title: Multi-channel EEG recordings during a sustained-attention driving task
description: This dataset comprises multi-channel EEG recordings from 27 healthy adults performing a sustained-attention driving task in a virtual-reality simulator across 62 sessions. The task involves event-related lane-departure paradigm where participants maintain vehicle position on a simulated highway, with recordings capturing 32-channel EEG (30 scalp + 2 mastoid references) sampled at 500 Hz alongside vehicle position data. The dataset includes approximately 82 hours of raw, unfiltered EEG data with 27,192 lane-departure trials and associated behavioral markers, providing a resource for studying attention, fatigue, and drowsiness during sustained driving tasks.
methods_description: EEG was acquired using a Compumedics Neuroscan Scan SynAmps2 Express amplifier with a 32-channel Quik-Cap (Ag/AgCl electrodes) at 500 Hz sampling rate with 16-bit quantization. Electrodes followed a modified international 10-20 system (30 scalp + 2 mastoid references A1/A2). Electrode-skin impedance was maintained below 5 kOhm using NaCl conductive gel and isopropyl-alcohol skin preparation. Participants performed a virtual-reality driving task on a six-degree-of-freedom Stewart motion platform with a real Ford Probe car frame, maintaining a vehicle on a simulated night-time highway while responding to randomly induced left/right lane-departure events. Data were originally acquired between 2005 and 2012 and converted to BIDS-EEG format using mne-bids.
